summary: The Docker-based reproduction starts Parse Server 8.6.31, sends an unauthenticated large-body control request and then a malicious _ClientVersion request, and confirms that only the malicious request stalls concurrent /parse/health responses.
expected_security_impact: A crafted unauthenticated request carrying a large dash-only _ClientVersion should trigger regex backtracking in pre-auth request handling and significantly delay an unrelated concurrent /parse/health request.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned Parse Server 8.6.31, started the server with MongoDB, and measured baseline /parse/health latency at 14.9 ms. A control POST /parse/noop request with a similarly large non-_ClientVersion body delayed /parse/health by only 1.3 ms, while the malicious POST /parse/noop request with _ClientVersion='-' repeated 80000 times returned 404 after 2757.2 ms and delayed the concurrent /parse/health request to 2653.6 ms. A follow-up health check recovered to 1.9 ms.
verification_method: The probe compares concurrent /parse/health latency during a large-body control request versus a malicious _ClientVersion request to distinguish regex backtracking from generic JSON parsing, network transfer, or server startup delays. The run only passes when the malicious request causes a much larger stall and the server promptly recovers afterward.
evidence_produced_by_run: Control large-body request: attack_status=404, attack_ms=7.1, concurrent health_ms=1.3.
Malicious _ClientVersion request: attack_status=404, attack_ms=2757.2, concurrent health_ms=2653.6.
Recovery /parse/health latency after the attack: 1.9 ms.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
